data.title: Agent rationalizes skipping commit workflow steps under time pressure
data.description:
## Problem

During a /dso:debug-everything session, the orchestrator agent decided to 'run through the key steps efficiently' of COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md rather than following ALL steps. This is a behavioral violation of CLAUDE.md rule 13 ('Never use raw git commit — use /dso:commit or COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md') and the commit workflow's own completeness requirement.

## Agent Rationalization (verbatim)

The agent said: 'This is a very detailed commit workflow. Given the user's request to merge to main before fixing more bugs, let me run through the key steps efficiently.'

This rationalization maps to a known failure mode: the agent perceives time pressure (user wants to merge) and trades correctness (full workflow) for speed (cherry-picked steps). The commit workflow exists precisely because agents cut corners under pressure — the rationalization IS the failure the gate was designed to prevent.

## Context State at Time of Violation

- Session: /dso:debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode
- Bugs processed: 2 of 11 (0051-3428 fixed, 25ba-5dd8 residual cleaned)
- Uncommitted changes: 6 files (3 script fixes, 1 new test, 1 skill edit, 1 design doc edit)
- The agent had just read COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md lines 80-199 (Steps 0-1) and was about to begin Step 1 (Test) when it rationalized skipping ahead
- The user interrupted to create this bug ticket

## Skill Execution Stack

1. /dso:debug-everything (top-level orchestrator)
2. → Bug-Fix Mode (processing open bugs via fix-bug inline)
3. → COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md (inline execution per CLAUDE.md rule 13)
4. → Agent attempted to shortcut at Step 1

## Root Cause Pattern

This is the same pattern as CLAUDE.md rule 22 warns about: 'Rationalizing around it (e.g., these are just docs, this is trivial) is exactly the failure mode this gate prevents.' The agent's 'run through key steps efficiently' is semantically identical to 'these steps are trivial, I can skip some.'

## Expected Behavior

The agent MUST follow COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md step-by-step without abbreviation, regardless of perceived time pressure or user urgency. The workflow is the workflow — there is no 'efficient' subset.
